summarization.a: The figure shows GI-XRD diffractograms, log intensity against 2θ for different values of x, cation ratio of  Ge doped tin oxide TGO
summarization.b: The figure shows GI-XRD diffractograms, log intensity against 2θ for different values of x, cation ratio of  Ge doped Zinc oxide ZGO

b.question: How does doping influence the crystallinity of ZGO?
b.answer: From GI-XRD, the low-Ge content ZGO films had wurtzite ZnO peaks that gradually weakened as the amount of Ge was increased (b). Increasing the Ge content further gave X-ray amorphous films

b.question: Where all the films examined, deposited on crystalline silicon substrate?

a.question: How is the structure of ALD GeOy?
a.answer: GeOy appears amorphous in grazing incidence X-ray diffraction (GI-XRD) diffractograms similar to GaOy and SnOy grown by ALD at the same temperature using metal−organic precursors with the same dimethylamine (DMA) ligand

a.question: Why is the amorphous nature of TGO not surprising?
a.answer: The GI-XRD diffractograms (a) showed an amorphous morphology for the SnOy and TGO  0.30 films. It is not surprising to see that TGO 0.3 was both resistive and X-ray amorphous, given that its growth consists of subcycles of SnOy and GeOy that also share these properties
